worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's empirical finding that Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x text tokens and up to 3x image tokens at the same per-token price is the most concrete cost signal in the feed — directly actionable for anyone building on the API.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost is resolution-dependent, not universal — prevents product teams from over-indexing on the scary headline number.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Visual reverse-engineering of Claude Code's 512k-line codebase into a readable map is genuinely useful for anyone trying to understand or orchestrate the agent loop.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router claiming 70% cost reduction for agents by routing cheap vs. expensive tasks — directly relevant to AI product cost strategy.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's counterfactual that OpenAI could have kept reasoners secret and avoided the DeepSeek moment is a sharp lens on how open publication shapes competitive dynamics.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The '30-day roadmap = ngmi' take (86k views, 67 reposts) is a crisp articulation of the planning horizon problem for indie devs and product teams in the current AI cycle.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The 'every paradigm must be reinvented for agents' thread has real strategic framing for thinking about where agent-native products will disrupt incumbent SaaS.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Gallup Q1 2026: 50% of employed Americans now use AI at work, up from 21% in 2023 — a milestone data point for sales narratives around AI adoption.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Real-world report of Opus 4.7 giving confidently wrong answers and forcing a rollback to 4.6 — an important reliability signal for anyone evaluating model switching in production.

voices.why: Doing actual empirical token-cost measurements on Opus 4.7 with tooling he publishes — the most grounded technical voice in this feed for API cost strategy.
Sharp, high-engagement takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution that are consistently relevant to indie dev and AI product planning.
Historically grounded perspective on AI competitive dynamics; the reasoner-as-missed-moat thread is the kind of strategic framing worth tracking.

examples: @aakashgupta posting ~30 posts of off-topic trivia (NASA, tennis longevity, Nolan films, artillery shells, Ariana Grande)
@RoundtableSpace all-caps crypto/trading bot hype and engagement-bait ('GM CT', 'WHAT ARE WE APING IN')
@DataChaz reposting jailbreak spam and motivational quote filler
